title: Robotics and embodied agents

publication.changeSummary: Source-linked AI Foundations draft for Robotics and embodied agents; all claims, examples, metrics, code, safety boundaries, and answer oracles require independent human review. Beginner-quality contract 1.0.0 applied deterministically from existing source-linked draft facts; publication stage and reviewer state are unchanged.

canonicalExplanation.summary: A robotic system links sensing, state estimation, planning, control, and actuation in a physical feedback loop with explicit safe states, limits, human roles, and fallback behavior.

sections.heading: Start here
sections.body: Start with this case: A mobile robot slows on uncertain localization, stops on sensor disagreement, and requires human clearance before leaving a mapped area. Your goal is to map an embodied task into perception, state, planning and action components with fallback behavior. Work through the example one step at a time, then use the boundary to check your answer.

sections.heading: Before you begin
sections.body: Review these declared prerequisites first: Neural networks and learned representations; Knowledge representation and uncertainty; Agent, goal and utility models; Actions, preconditions and effects; Computer vision and perception. If one is unfamiliar, revisit it before continuing.

sections.heading: Bounded operational model
sections.body: A robotic system links sensing, state estimation, planning, control, and actuation in a physical feedback loop with explicit safe states, limits, human roles, and fallback behavior. Any capability or quality claim remains limited to an explicit task, population, conditions, baseline, and recorded evidence.

sections.heading: Failure boundary and non-claim
sections.body: Simulation omits friction and rare events, sensors fail, latency destabilizes control, plans can be valid but unsafe, physical actions are not instantly reversible, and learning must remain inside safety constraints. The learner must reject this overclaim: A planner or learned policy that succeeds in simulation can control a real robot safely once sensors are connected.

includes: Connect sensing, localization, planning, control and actuation under physical safety constraints.
Map an embodied task into perception, state, planning and action components with fallback behavior.
excludes: Simulation omits friction and rare events, sensors fail, latency destabilizes control, plans can be valid but unsafe, physical actions are not instantly reversible, and learning must remain inside safety constraints.

outcomes: Map an embodied task into perception, state, planning and action components with fallback behavior.

examples.title: Robotics and embodied agents: bounded direct case
examples.situation: A mobile robot slows on uncertain localization, stops on sensor disagreement, and requires human clearance before leaving a mapped area.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Observe the exact data, state, metric, or tool behavior: A mobile robot slows on uncertain localization, stops on sensor disagreement, and requires human clearance before leaving a mapped area.
Step 2 — Use the core rule above to predict one observable result.

Step 3 — Verify the claim boundary with: Validate the complete perception-to-actuation loop under bounded physical tests, enforce independent safety limits, and define stop, fallback, and human escalation paths.
examples.result: This example supports only the atomic outcome because Validate the complete perception-to-actuation loop under bounded physical tests, enforce independent safety limits, and define stop, fallback, and human escalation paths.

examples.title: Robotics and embodied agents: stress and transfer case
examples.situation: A simulation policy scores well but collides after wheel slip, so hardware testing proceeds through bounded speed and emergency-stop stages.
walkthrough: Step 1 — Name the changed population, input, assumption, or failure: A simulation policy scores well but collides after wheel slip, so hardware testing proceeds through bounded speed and emergency-stop stages.

Step 2 — Keep the non-claim visible: Simulation omits friction and rare events, sensors fail, latency destabilizes control, plans can be valid but unsafe, physical actions are not instantly reversible, and learning must remain inside safety constraints.
Step 3 — Reject the tempting overclaim: A planner or learned policy that succeeds in simulation can control a real robot safely once sensors are connected.
examples.result: This case demonstrates the declared outcome: Map an embodied task into perception, state, planning and action components with fallback behavior.

misconceptions.mistakenBelief: A planner or learned policy that succeeds in simulation can control a real robot safely once sensors are connected.

misconceptions.diagnosticPrompt: Identify the first unsupported inference in this belief and the evidence required to narrow it: A planner or learned policy that succeeds in simulation can control a real robot safely once sensors are connected.

analogy.example: a sensing-and-decision pipeline that names modality inputs, task outputs, affected populations, operating conditions, and fallback paths can introduce Robotics and embodied agents only after its canonical technical definition and evidence boundary are stated.
usefulFor: NLP, vision, robotics, and multimodal evaluation
limitations: more modalities or fluent outputs do not establish understanding, truth, physical safety, or universal coverage

recap.summary: You should now be able to map an embodied task into perception, state, planning and action components with fallback behavior. Keep this boundary in view: Simulation omits friction and rare events, sensors fail, latency destabilizes control, plans can be valid but unsafe, physical actions are not instantly reversible, and learning must remain inside safety constraints.
retrievalPrompts: Explain Robotics and embodied agents with one technical mechanism, one evidence requirement, and one non-claim.

Apply the boundary to: A simulation policy scores well but collides after wheel slip, so hardware testing proceeds through bounded speed and emergency-stop stages.
